content:
On Sunday morning, Finnish air‑spacesurveillance detected several low‑flying, slow‑moving small objects over sea areas and southeastern Finland. The Finnish Air Force dispatched an F/A‑18 Hornet fighter to the scene to identify the objects. According to the latest information, one of the objects — identified as a drone — crashed north of Kouvola, while a second drone crashed east of Kouvola. Police have secured both crash sites for further investigation.  

Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen commented, “Drones have strayed into Finnish territory. We take this very seriously. Security authorities have responded immediately.”  

The Defence Forces are now conducting a joint investigation of the incident together with other relevant authorities.
